title: Masquerade Shader Forge
description: A venetian mask floats center-stage wearing enchanted sunglasses. The user manipulates custom shader effects on both objects through a multi-step ritual: cycling through procedural shader modes (holographic, dissolve, energy pulse, chromatic aberration, x-ray), adjusting shader intensity, toggling per-object shader isolation, and triggering a final fusion shader that merges both objects' visual fields into a unified psychedelic effect.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (importmap with cdn.jsdelivr.net) that loads venice_mask.glb and SunglassesKhronos.glb via GLTFLoader. Position the venice mask at scene center facing the camera. Attach the sunglasses onto the mask's face region, slightly forward on Z so they sit naturally on the bridge area. Use a dark background (0x0a0a1a) with subtle ambient light and one directional light from upper-right.

Implement custom ShaderMaterial replacements applied to both models' meshes. Create 5 shader modes the user cycles through: (1) Holographic — vertex-displaced iridescent rainbow using view angle and time uniform, (2) Dissolve — noise-based alpha cutoff that eats away geometry edges with glowing orange borders, (3) Energy Pulse — radial sine waves emanating from object centers with electric blue glow, (4) Chromatic Aberration — fragment offset splitting RGB channels based on distance from center, (5) X-Ray — fresnel-based transparency showing wireframe-like edges in neon green. All shaders must use a shared time uniform updated each frame.

Interaction steps with validation: (Step 1) Press 1-5 keys to select shader mode — display current mode name in an on-screen HUD div. (Step 2) Use a mouse wheel to adjust a 'shaderIntensity' uniform from 0.0 to 2.0, shown numerically on HUD. (Step 3) Press M to toggle shader application between 'mask only', 'glasses only', or 'both' — validate and display target on HUD. (Step 4) Press F to toggle a fusion mode that blends both objects' shader outputs using an additive post-process pass via EffectComposer with a custom ShaderPass. (Step 5) Press R to animate a slow Y-axis rotation toggle on the combined group. (Step 6) Press Space to capture current parameters and log them to console as a JSON snapshot including mode, intensity, target, fusion state, and rotation state. Validate all parameters are within expected ranges before logging. OrbitControls should be enabled for camera manipulation. The HUD should show all current states persistently in the top-left corner with a semi-transparent dark background.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  currentShaderMode: number,         // current shader mode index (1-5)
  shaderIntensity: number,         // shader effect intensity multiplier (0.0-2.0)
  postProcessingEnabled: boolean,         // whether post-processing bloom is active
  maskRotationSpeed: number,         // rotation speed of venice mask in radians per second
  sunglassesAttached: boolean,         // whether sunglasses are attached to mask
  timeUniform: number,         // shared time uniform value for all shaders
  ambientLightIntensity: number,         // ambient light intensity level
  directionalLightIntensity: number,         // directional light intensity level
  cameraDistance: number,         // camera distance from scene center
  animationActive: boolean,         // whether shader animation loop is running
}
